problem.summary: Duration Stability shows 0% for single runs (lastRunOnly=true), contributing +0 to reliability score when it should be +30
problem.root_cause: Current formula calculates coefficient of variation (STDDEV/AVG) across all test durations within ONE run. This measures "uniformity of test durations" not "stability over time."
problem.symptom: A run with tests ranging 2s-60s gets high CV = low stability (incorrect)
problem.expected_behavior: For a single run with no historical data to compare against, stability should be 100% (no regression detected)

task.objective: Investigate and plan proper fix for duration stability calculation

success_criteria.criterion: Identify all files involved in stability calculation
Understand current formula and its flaws
Design correct stability metric for single vs multi-run scenarios

step.name: Locate Stability Calculation
step.action: Find where duration stability is calculated (API, lib, components)
step.output: List of files with stability logic

step.name: Analyze Current Formula
step.action: Document the current STDDEV/AVG approach and why it fails

step.name: Design Correct Metric
step.action: Define proper stability calculation for single-run and multi-run scenarios

step.name: Create Phase Prompts
step.action: Generate implementation phases in phases/ folder
